question: Comment implementer le distributed tracing pour suivre les requetes a travers plusieurs services ?

principles.text: Propager le contexte de trace via le header HTTP `traceparent` (W3C Trace Context) : ce standard garantit l'interoperabilite entre tous les services et outils sans lock-in. Format : `00-{traceId 32hex}-{spanId 16hex}-{flags}`. Ne jamais inventer un format proprietaire.

sources.name: W3C Trace Context Level 1 Recommendation
sources.quote: traceparent header MUST be forwarded by intermediaries; defines traceId + spanId + flags

sources.name: W3C Trace Context Level 2 Candidate Recommendation
sources.quote: Extends Level 1 with additional flags and clarifications

sources.name: NIST SP 800-204A — Building Secure Microservices-based Applications
sources.quote: Distributed tracing via correlation headers is required for latency metrics in microservice architectures

sources.name: OpenTelemetry Tracing API Specification (CNCF)
sources.quote: W3C Trace Context is the default propagator

sources.name: Dapper — a Large-Scale Distributed Systems Tracing Infrastructure (Google)
sources.quote: Context propagation across service boundaries is the foundational primitive of distributed tracing

principles.text: Utiliser OpenTelemetry comme couche d'instrumentation universelle : traces + spans + propagation de contexte via une API stable et vendor-agnostique. OTel est le standard CNCF pour l'observabilite — evite le lock-in sur Jaeger, Zipkin ou Datadog.

sources.name: OpenTelemetry Tracing API Specification (CNCF)
sources.quote: Stable API — Tracer, Span, Context, Sampler, SpanProcessor, SpanExporter

sources.name: OTLP Specification 1.10.0 (CNCF)
sources.quote: Ports canoniques : 4317 (gRPC), 4318 (HTTP/protobuf)

sources.name: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions (CNCF)
sources.quote: Standardized attribute naming for http.method, db.system, rpc.service, etc.

sources.name: Jaeger v2 (CNCF Graduated)
sources.quote: Jaeger v2 deprecates proprietary SDKs and adopts OTel Collector — OTel is the canonical instrumentation path

sources.name: Dapper — a Large-Scale Distributed Systems Tracing Infrastructure (Google)
sources.quote: Trace/Span/Context model — concepts fondateurs adoptes par OTel

principles.text: Transmettre des donnees contextuelles cross-service via le header W3C Baggage (paires cle=valeur) : user-id, tenant-id, request-id. Ne jamais mettre de PII ou de secrets dans Baggage — le header est propagé en clair sur toute la chaine.

sources.name: W3C Baggage Candidate Recommendation
sources.quote: Baggage header propagates key-value context; no PII — visible to all intermediaries

sources.name: OpenTelemetry Tracing API Specification (CNCF)
sources.quote: Baggage API is part of the OTel context propagation API

principles.text: Choisir la strategie de sampling selon le trafic : head-based (decision a l'entree, simple, 1-10% en prod) pour les volumes eleves ; tail-based (decision apres reception de la trace complete, necessite un OTel Collector avec buffer) pour capturer systematiquement les erreurs et les traces lentes. Ne JAMAIS tracer 100% en production — l'overhead est quantifie.

sources.name: OpenTelemetry Sampling Concepts (CNCF)
sources.quote: Head-based vs tail-based sampling — fundamental tradeoff between simplicity and completeness

sources.name: OTel Collector Architecture (CNCF)
sources.quote: Tail-based sampling requires the Collector as buffer; head-based works without Collector

sources.name: Nõu et al. — Overhead of Distributed Tracing in Microservice Architectures (ICPE 2025, ACM/SPEC)
sources.quote: Throughput -19% a -80%, latency +175% selon config de sampling — sampling indispensable en production

sources.name: OTel Performance Benchmark Specification (CNCF)
sources.quote: Methodologie officielle de mesure overhead : benchmark trace vs no-trace sous charge

principles.text: Instrumenter les acces base de donnees dans les traces : chaque requete DB doit generer un span fils avec les attributs `db.system`, `db.statement` (sanitize, sans valeurs), `db.operation`. Sans cela, les goulots DB sont invisibles depuis la trace end-to-end.

sources.name: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions (CNCF)
sources.quote: db.system, db.statement, db.operation — semantic conventions for database spans

sources.name: Liu et al. — Database Performance Analysis via Distributed Tracing (MDPI Applied Sciences)
sources.quote: DB contribution to end-to-end latency is measurable only when DB spans are included in the trace

principles.text: En l'absence de tracing : impossible de diagnostiquer les latences P99 cross-service. Un seul service lent noie les logs des autres. Le tracing n'est pas un luxe — c'est l'instrument de diagnostic des architectures multi-services (correlation end-to-end que les logs par service ne peuvent pas fournir seuls).

sources.name: NIST SP 800-204A — Building Secure Microservices-based Applications
Sridharan — Distributed Systems Observability (O'Reilly)
sources.quote: Traces are the third pillar of observability — logs and metrics alone cannot answer 'why is this request slow?'

sources.name: Shkuro — Mastering Distributed Tracing (Packt)
sources.quote: Without end-to-end trace correlation, cross-service latency is undiagnosable

sources.name: Eaton et al. — Critical Path Tracing at Google (ACM Queue / CACM)
sources.quote: Critical path analysis from production traces — identifies bottlenecks invisible to per-service metrics

sources.name: Li et al. — Tracing Practices in Industry (Empirical Software Engineering)
sources.quote: Survey de 10 entreprises — le tracing est adopte comme outil principal de diagnostic de latence P99

typescript-nestjs.recommendation: Utiliser `@opentelemetry/instrumentation-nestjs-core` (auto-instrumentation officielle OTel pour NestJS) + initialisation SDK OTel AVANT tout import NestJS dans le fichier d'entree (ordre obligatoire — sinon les decorateurs NestJS ne sont pas interceptes). Exporter vers Jaeger ou Tempo via OTLP (port 4317 gRPC ou 4318 HTTP). Packages : `@opentelemetry/sdk-node`, `@opentelemetry/auto-instrumentations-node`, `@opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-grpc`.

sources.name: @opentelemetry/instrumentation-nestjs-core (npm, OTel officiel)
sources.quote: Official auto-instrumentation for NestJS — instruments guards, interceptors, pipes, controllers

sources.name: OTel Node.js Getting Started (CNCF)
sources.quote: SDK initialization MUST happen before any NestJS imports — order is critical

sources.name: OTLP Specification 1.10.0 (CNCF)
sources.quote: Port 4317 (gRPC), 4318 (HTTP/protobuf) — ports canoniques OTLP

sources.name: Jaeger v2 (CNCF Graduated)
sources.quote: Backend de reference CNCF; accepte OTLP nativement depuis v2

java-spring-boot.recommendation: Utiliser Spring Boot Actuator + Micrometer Tracing (bridge OTel) ou l'agent Java OTel (`opentelemetry-javaagent.jar`) en mode zero-code. L'agent instrumente automatiquement JDBC, RestTemplate/WebClient, Spring MVC. Exporter vers Jaeger ou Tempo via OTLP. Le bridge Micrometer Tracing est prefere pour l'integration Spring native ; l'agent Java est prefere si le codebase n'est pas modifiable.

sources.name: OpenTelemetry Java Agent (CNCF)
sources.quote: Zero-code instrumentation for JVM — instruments Spring MVC, JDBC, HTTP clients automatically

sources.name: Spring Boot Actuator + Micrometer Tracing docs
sources.quote: Micrometer Tracing bridges OTel SDK — native Spring integration since Spring Boot 3.x

python-django.recommendation: Utiliser l'auto-instrumentation OTel Python : `opentelemetry-instrument` CLI ou `opentelemetry-instrumentation-django`. Exporter vers Jaeger ou Tempo via OTLP. Packages : `opentelemetry-sdk`, `opentelemetry-instrumentation-django`, `opentelemetry-exporter-otlp`.

sources.name: OpenTelemetry Python Instrumentation (CNCF)
sources.quote: opentelemetry-instrumentation-django — auto-instruments Django requests and DB calls
